atoms.source_hunk_hint: @@ -21,7 +21,7 @@ use crate::execute::BasicBlockBuilder; and @@ -290,6 +332,10 @@ pub struct NextBlockEnvAttributes
atoms.semantic_claim: The downstream Reth next-block environment gained per-block extra_data input, represented as an optional Bytes override, so payload building can carry dynamic block header extra_data instead of always relying on the assembler default.

destinations.evidence: NextBlockEnvAttributes imports Bytes and defines pub extra_data: Bytes at lines 24 and 493-494; the v2 type is mandatory Bytes rather than Option<Bytes>.
Payload building populates NextBlockEnvAttributes { extra_data: builder_config.extra_data, ... } before constructing the EVM/block execution context.
context_for_next_block forwards attributes.extra_data into EthBlockExecutionCtx.extra_data, which is later used by block assembly.
EthereumBuilderConfig owns extra_data: Bytes and exposes with_extra_data, giving the v2 port a builder-level source for the required field.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination preserves dynamic header extra_data flow, but the representation changed from an optional override to an always-present Bytes value sourced from builder configuration or parent/pending defaults. This is behaviorally close but not exact because the old None/default distinction is collapsed into Bytes::new or an explicit caller-provided value.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Build a payload with non-empty builder extra_data and assert the assembled header carries those exact bytes, then build with the default empty value and assert no stale prior extra_data is reused.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: non-empty and default extra_data flow is covered by mandatory Bytes in NextBlockEnvAttributes plus EthereumBuilderConfig.with_extra_data.

atoms.source_hunk_hint: @@ -29,9 +29,19 @@ use reth_primitives_traits and @@ -165,9 +178,20 @@ pub trait ConfigureEvm
atoms.semantic_claim: When the async-context feature is enabled, every EVM created through ConfigureEvm::evm_with_env gets a fresh AsyncCommitmentutionContext installed before execution proceeds.

destinations.evidence: The reth-evm crate keeps async-context as a default feature and forwards it to alloy-evm, revm, and reth-execution-errors.

RitualEvmFactory::create_evm and create_evm_with_inspector create a revm_context::async_context::AsyncCommitmentutionContext::default() and install it through AsyncEthEvmContext::with_async_context before returning RitualEvm.

AsyncEthEvmContext stores an optional boxed AsyncCommitmentutionContext under cfg(feature = "async-context") and implements AsyncContextTr by returning that context or panicking if it was not installed.
The revm context crate defines AsyncContextTr with async_context and async_context_mut accessors, which is the trait surface used by Ritual's custom EVM context.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination no longer mutates a generic Reth EVM immediately after ConfigureEvm::evm_with_env. Instead, Ritual's nodebuilder-owned EVM factory constructs an async-aware context and installs the default async context before the EVM is returned. This preserves the fresh-context invariant for the Ritual EVM path, but it is non-exact because the behavior moved from the generic reth-evm ConfigureEvm default method into the custom RitualEvmFactory/AsyncEthEvmContext path.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Execute an async-context precompile through the nodebuilder EVM factory and assert AsyncContextTr is available without panic; also run the same route with an inspector to cover create_evm_with_inspector.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: fresh async context is installed by RitualEvmFactory/AsyncEthEvmContext for the active Ritual EVM path; non-exact but covered.

atoms.semantic_claim: EVMs created for existing-block execution/verification are explicitly switched to async-context Verification mode before block transactions are executed.

destinations.evidence: RitualBlockExecutorProvider installs AsyncContextFactory::verification_context(*txn.tx_hash(), spc_calls.clone()) into strategy.evm_mut().ctx_mut().async_context_mut() before executing each transaction during block execution.
The state-hook block execution path also installs AsyncContextFactory::verification_context for each transaction before strategy.execute_transaction(tx).
AsyncContextFactory::verification_context constructs AsyncCommitmentutionContext::with_mode(ExecutionMode::Verification), sets the transaction hash, and preloads any SPC calls.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination preserves verification-mode execution for block verification, but it does so per transaction and includes tx_hash/SPC-call hydration rather than setting a single block-level mode in ConfigureEvm::evm_for_block. This is structurally stronger for transaction-local async validation but non-exact relative to the old generic hook.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Verify a block containing one async/SPC transaction followed by one non-async transaction and assert both execute in Verification mode with no leaked pending_spc_calls between transactions.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: verification mode is installed per transaction through AsyncContextFactory::verification_context, with tx_hash/SPC-call hydration. Covered structurally.

atoms.source_hunk_hint: @@ -259,6 +292,7 @@ pub trait ConfigureEvm and @@ -266,7 +300,15 @@ pub trait ConfigureEvm
atoms.semantic_claim: EVMs used for next-block payload building are switched to async-context Building mode before block builder creation, so async precompiles collect/build rather than verify fulfilled async calls.

destinations.evidence: RitualEvmFactory initializes AsyncCommitmentutionContext::default() for created EVMs; this is the EVM factory used by EthEvmConfig<RitualEvmFactory> in the nodebuilder path.
AsyncCommitmentutionContext::default() uses ExecutionMode::default(), and the revm-context tests assert ExecutionMode::default() and AsyncCommitmentutionContext::new() are Building.

The v2 payload builder creates an EVM through evm_config.evm_with_env_and_inspector and then creates the block builder with create_block_builder, using the nodebuilder-provided Ritual EVM factory when installed.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination preserves Building mode for payload-building EVMs through the default async context installed by RitualEvmFactory rather than by an explicit builder_for_next_block assignment. This is non-exact because the default-mode invariant is implicit and the v2 payload path often bypasses builder_for_next_block by calling evm_with_env_and_inspector plus create_block_builder directly.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: During payload construction, execute a Ritual async precompile that behaves differently in Building versus Verification mode and assert the building skip/collection path is taken rather than the verification-unavailable path.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: payload-building uses the default Building mode installed by RitualEvmFactory and revm-context defaults. Covered structurally.

atoms.semantic_claim: The downstream reth-evm crate publicly exposes std-gated pool_manager and spc modules so block-verification code can access synchronous async-system transaction pool removal and SPC verification trait abstractions without circular dependencies.

destinations.evidence: The latest port commit restores #[cfg(feature = "std")] pub mod spc; in reth_evm.
PoolInner::process_async_commitment_transactions runs during on_canonical_state_change, moves canonical async commitments into async lifecycle metadata, and prunes original transaction hashes.
verify_async_commitments validates decoded origin hashes and active-job state; verify_nonce_locks rejects duplicate commitments and active-job sender conflicts.
canonical_async_commitment_moves_original_to_async_pool asserts original removal, async metadata, and sender locking after a commitment becomes canonical.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The SPC trait surface is now present. The old pool_manager module is not restored; its mutation intent is handled by canonical-state async pool transitions and verifier nonce-lock/origin checks rather than a global verifier-time singleton.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run a cargo check for the nodebuilder executor against the v2 Reth workspace and confirm reth_evm::spc resolves; separately verify async system transaction pool removal is either intentionally replaced or still removes originals during verification.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: stale missing status fixed. reth_evm::spc exists; pool_manager is a legacy compatibility surface intentionally absent/replaced by canonical-state handling.

atoms.semantic_claim: The downstream reth-evm crate gained a cfg(test) async_context_tests module intended to exercise async context wiring around EVM creation and execution modes.

destinations.evidence: revm-context has cfg(all(test, feature = "async-context")) tests for AsyncContext and AsyncContextTr behavior, including building and verification mode assertions.
ONNX verification tests assert AsyncCommitmentutionContext::verification() is Verification mode and distinguish building skip signals from verification-unavailable signals.
JQ verification tests assert building versus verification mode behavior and signal separation for async-context timeout paths.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination has async-context tests in the revm dependency and nodebuilder precompile crate rather than an exact reth-evm async_context_tests module. These tests cover core mode and signal behavior but do not directly prove the reth-evm ConfigureEvm wiring from the source patch, so the mapping is only structural.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Add or run a destination test that constructs the RitualEvmFactory through the Reth EVM config and asserts both plain and inspector EVM creation install AsyncContextTr with Building mode by default, plus verification context swapping before block execution.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: exact mod async_context_tests is absent, but the relevant assertions are dependency-local tests or file-0106 test-only gaps.

summary: Mechanical review complete. Header extra_data and async-context Building/Verification wiring are covered structurally across the v2 port, nodebuilder, and revm-context. The latest commit restores reth_evm::spc. The old pool_manager module and exact async_context_tests module are absent as compatibility/test surfaces; no confirmed FSM/runtime gap remains.
notes: Reviewed against latest code. file-0108-atom-0005 was stale: reth_evm::spc now exists after 7f8a668d24; only the legacy pool_manager module surface remains intentionally absent/replaced by file-0110 canonical-state design.
